exercises.title: Welcome to Chapter 3
exercises.body: You can read every letter and build simple sentences. This chapter is about real Shavian text — the endings, everyday words and dialogue that fill actual pages. Everything is still spelled by sound; you just haven’t met all its habits yet.

exercises.title: Plurals follow your voice
exercises.body: Say “cats”, then “dogs”. The s buzzes in dogs but not in cats — so Shavian writes cats 𐑒𐑨𐑑𐑕 and dogs 𐑛𐑪𐑜𐑟. There is no one-size “-s” to memorize: write the sound you actually make.

exercises.caption: Build this word from Shavian letters

exercises.title: An extra beat: 𐑩𐑟
exercises.body: After a hissy sound — s, z, sh, ch, j — you can’t just stack on another hiss. English slips in a tiny vowel, and Shavian writes it: boxes is 𐑚𐑪𐑒𐑕𐑩𐑟, wishes is 𐑢𐑦𐑖𐑩𐑟.

exercises.caption: Sound it out — what does this say?
Sound it out — what does this say?

exercises.title: -ing is always 𐑦𐑙
exercises.body: The “-ing” ending never changes: running is 𐑮𐑳𐑯𐑦𐑙, reading is 𐑮𐑰𐑛𐑦𐑙, morning is 𐑥𐑹𐑯𐑦𐑙. Two letters, every time.

exercises.caption: Sound it out — what does this say?
Build this word from Shavian letters

exercises.title: New: spot the word
exercises.body: From now on you’ll sometimes get a whole Shavian sentence and one word to find in it. Read the sentence, then tap the word that matches. That’s reading in the wild — scanning real text for meaning.
